| `attachments` | [Attachment](#attachment)\[]     | No       | File attachments                                                            |

### Example Request

```bash
curl -X POST https://app.northrelay.ca/api/v1/emails/send \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer nr_live_xxxxxxxxxxxx" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "from": { "email": "hello@yourdomain.com", "name": "Acme Inc" },
    "to": [
      { "email": "alice@example.com", "name": "Alice" }
    ],
    "replyTo": { "email": "support@yourdomain.com" },
    "content": {
      "subject": "Your order has shipped",
      "html": "<h1>Order #1234</h1><p>Your package is on the way.</p>",
      "text": "Order #1234 - Your package is on the way."
    },
    "categories": ["transactional", "order-updates"],
    "tags": { "order_id": "1234" },
    "metadata": { "internal_ref": "ship-batch-42" }
  }'
```

### Example Response

```json
{
  "id": "msg_a1b2c3d4e5f6",
  "status": "queued",
  "from": "hello@yourdomain.com",
  "to": ["alice@example.com"],
  "subject": "Your order has shipped",
  "createdAt": "2026-03-19T14:30:00.000Z"
}
```

***

## Batch Send

Send personalized emails to up to 1,000 recipients in a single request. Each recipient can have unique variable substitutions.

```
POST /emails/batch
```

### Request Body

| Field        | Type                                 | Required | Description                                                |
| ------------ | ------------------------------------ | -------- | ---------------------------------------------------------- |
| `from`       | [EmailAddress](#emailaddress)        | Yes      | Sender address (shared across all recipients)              |
| `recipients` | [BatchRecipient](#batchrecipient)\[] | Yes      | Recipients with per-recipient variables (1--1,000)         |
| `content`    | [Content](#content)                  | Yes      | Shared email content; `subject` is required (not optional) |
| `categories` | string\[]                            | No       | Up to 10 categories                                        |
| `tags`       | Record\<string, string>              | No       | Custom key-value tags                                      |
| `metadata`   | Record\<string, any>                 | No       | Arbitrary metadata                                         |

### BatchRecipient Object

| Field       | Type                             | Required | Description                      |
| ----------- | -------------------------------- | -------- | -------------------------------- |
| `to`        | [EmailAddress](#emailaddress)    | Yes      | Recipient address                |
| `cc`        | [EmailAddress](#emailaddress)\[] | No       | CC recipients for this message   |
| `bcc`       | [EmailAddress](#emailaddress)\[] | No       | BCC recipients for this message  |
| `variables` | Record\<string, string>          | No       | Per-recipient template variables |

### Example Request

```bash
curl -X POST https://app.northrelay.ca/api/v1/emails/batch \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer nr_live_xxxxxxxxxxxx" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "from": { "email": "hello@yourdomain.com", "name": "Acme Inc" },
    "recipients": [
      {
        "to": { "email": "alice@example.com", "name": "Alice" },
        "variables": { "first_name": "Alice", "plan": "Startup" }
      },
      {
        "to": { "email": "bob@example.com", "name": "Bob" },
        "variables": { "first_name": "Bob", "plan": "Scale" }
      }
    ],
    "content": {
      "subject": "Welcome to {{plan}}, {{first_name}}!",
      "html": "<h1>Hi {{first_name}}</h1><p>You are now on the {{plan}} plan.</p>"
    },
    "categories": ["onboarding"]
  }'
```

### Example Response

```json
{
  "batchId": "batch_x1y2z3",
  "status": "queued",
  "totalRecipients": 2,
  "createdAt": "2026-03-19T14:30:00.000Z"
}
```

***

## Schedule Email

Schedule an email for future delivery. The request body is identical to [Send Email](#send-email) with one additional required field.

```
POST /emails/schedule
```

### Additional Field

| Field          | Type              | Required | Description                                            |
| -------------- | ----------------- | -------- | ------------------------------------------------------ |
| `scheduledFor` | string (ISO 8601) | Yes      | Delivery time; must be in the future and within 1 year |

### Example Request

```bash
curl -X POST https://app.northrelay.ca/api/v1/emails/schedule \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer nr_live_xxxxxxxxxxxx" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "from": { "email": "hello@yourdomain.com" },
    "to": [{ "email": "alice@example.com" }],
    "content": {
      "subject": "Your trial ends tomorrow",
      "html": "<p>Just a reminder that your trial expires on March 20.</p>"
    },
    "scheduledFor": "2026-03-20T09:00:00.000Z"
  }'
```

### Example Response

```json
{
  "id": "msg_s4c5h6e7d8",
  "status": "scheduled",
  "scheduledFor": "2026-03-20T09:00:00.000Z",
  "createdAt": "2026-03-19T14:30:00.000Z"
}
```

### Cancelling a Scheduled Email

```
DELETE /emails/schedule/{id}
```

Cancel a scheduled email before it is sent. Returns `404` if the email has already been delivered.

```bash
curl -X DELETE https://app.northrelay.ca/api/v1/emails/schedule/msg_s4c5h6e7d8 \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer nr_live_xxxxxxxxxxxx"
```

***

## Validate Email

Check whether an email address is deliverable without sending a message. Performs syntax validation, DNS MX lookup, and optionally SMTP handshake verification.

```
POST /emails/validate
```

### Request Body

| Field      | Type    | Required | Default | Description                                            |
| ---------- | ------- | -------- | ------- | ------------------------------------------------------ |
| `email`    | string  | Yes      | --      | Email address to validate                              |
| `extended` | boolean | No       | `false` | Enable SMTP handshake check (slower but more accurate) |
| `timeout`  | integer | No       | `10000` | Timeout in milliseconds (1,000--60,000)                |

### Example Request

```bash
curl -X POST https://app.northrelay.ca/api/v1/emails/validate \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer nr_live_xxxxxxxxxxxx" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "email": "alice@example.com",
    "extended": true,
    "timeout": 15000
  }'
```

### Example Response

```json
{
  "email": "alice@example.com",
  "valid": true,
  "checks": {
    "syntax": true,
    "mxRecords": true,
    "smtpHandshake": true,
    "disposable": false,
    "roleAccount": false
  },
  "suggestion": null
}
```

When validation fails, the `suggestion` field may offer a corrected address (e.g., `"suggestion": "alice@gmail.com"` for a misspelled domain).

### Bulk Validate

Validate up to 10,000 addresses in a single request.

```
POST /emails/validate/bulk
```

| Field             | Type      | Required | Default | Description                                          |
| ----------------- | --------- | -------- | ------- | ---------------------------------------------------- |
| `emails`          | string\[] | Yes      | --      | Email addresses to validate (1--10,000)              |
| `extended`        | boolean   | No       | `false` | Enable SMTP handshake checks                         |
| `timeout`         | integer   | No       | `30000` | Timeout in milliseconds (1,000--60,000)              |
| `continueOnError` | boolean   | No       | `true`  | Continue validating remaining addresses if one fails |

***

## Shared Types

### EmailAddress

| Field   | Type   | Required | Description         |
| ------- | ------ | -------- | ------------------- |
| `email` | string | Yes      | Valid email address |
| `name`  | string | No       | Display name        |

```json
{ "email": "alice@example.com", "name": "Alice Johnson" }
```

### Content

| Field         | Type                         | Required    | Description                                                            |
| ------------- | ---------------------------- | ----------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `subject`     | string                       | Conditional | Required unless `templateId` is provided (1--998 chars)                |
| `html`        | string                       | Conditional | HTML body; at least one of `html`, `text`, or `templateId` is required |
| `text`        | string                       | Conditional | Plain-text body                                                        |
| `templateId`  | string                       | Conditional | ID of a saved [template](/api-reference/templates)                     |
| `attachments` | [Attachment](#attachment)\[] | No          | File attachments                                                       |

### Attachment

| Field         | Type   | Required | Description                                    |
| ------------- | ------ | -------- | ---------------------------------------------- |
| `filename`    | string | Yes      | File name with extension (e.g., `invoice.pdf`) |
| `content`     | string | Yes      | Base64-encoded file content                    |
| `contentType` | string | Yes      | MIME type (e.g., `application/pdf`)            |

***

## Validation Rules Summary

| Constraint                  | Value                                            |
| --------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------ |
| Max recipients per send     | 1,000                                            |
| Max recipients per batch    | 1,000                                            |
| Max subject length          | 998 characters                                   |
| Max categories per email    | 10                                               |
| Category format             | Alphanumeric, hyphens, underscores; max 64 chars |
| Schedule window             | Must be in the future, within 1 year             |
| Max bulk validate addresses | 10,000                                           |
| Validate timeout range      | 1,000--60,000 ms                                 |


# Suppressions API



# Suppressions API

The Suppressions API manages addresses that should not receive emails. Suppressions prevent delivery to recipients who have bounced, complained, or explicitly unsubscribed. NorthRelay automatically adds addresses to the suppression list on hard bounces and spam complaints; you can also manage suppressions manually via the API.

## How Suppressions Work

When you send an email, NorthRelay checks the recipient address against your suppression list before queuing it. If the address is suppressed, the email is silently dropped and not counted against your monthly quota.

Suppression sources:

| Source        | Trigger                            | Removable    |
| ------------- | ---------------------------------- | ------------ |
| `bounce`      | Hard bounce (5xx SMTP response)    | Yes, via API |
| `complaint`   | Spam complaint via feedback loop   | Yes, via API |
| `manual`      | Added via API or dashboard         | Yes, via API |
| `unsubscribe` | Recipient clicked unsubscribe link | Yes, via API |

***

## List Suppressions

Retrieve all suppressed addresses.

```
GET /suppressions
```

### Query Parameters

| Parameter | Type    | Default | Description                                                      |
| --------- | ------- | ------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `page`    | integer | `1`     | Page number                                                      |
| `limit`   | integer | `25`    | Items per page (max 100)                                         |
| `source`  | string  | --      | Filter by source: `bounce`, `complaint`, `manual`, `unsubscribe` |
| `groupId` | string  | --      | Filter by suppression group ID                                   |
| `search`  | string  | --      | Search by email address                                          |

### Example Request

```bash
curl "https://app.northrelay.ca/api/v1/suppressions?source=bounce&limit=10" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer nr_live_xxxxxxxxxxxx"
```

### Example Response

```json
{
  "data": [
    {
      "id": "sup_a1b2c3d4",
      "email": "invalid@example.com",
      "source": "bounce",
      "reason": "550 5.1.1 The email account that you tried to reach does not exist",
      "groupId": null,
      "createdAt": "2026-03-10T08:15:00.000Z"
    },
    {
      "id": "sup_e5f6g7h8",
      "email": "nospam@example.com",
      "source": "complaint",
      "reason": "Spam complaint via feedback loop",
      "groupId": null,
      "createdAt": "2026-03-12T14:20:00.000Z"
    }
  ],
  "pagination": {
    "page": 1,
    "limit": 10,
    "total": 24,
    "totalPages": 3
  }
}
```

***

## Add Suppression

Manually suppress an email address.

```
POST /suppressions
```

### Request Body

| Field     | Type   | Required | Description                                         |
| --------- | ------ | -------- | --------------------------------------------------- |
| `email`   | string | Yes      | Email address to suppress                           |
| `reason`  | string | No       | Reason for suppression                              |
| `groupId` | string | No       | Suppression group ID for category-based suppression |

### Example Request

```bash
curl -X POST https://app.northrelay.ca/api/v1/suppressions \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer nr_live_xxxxxxxxxxxx" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "email": "optout@example.com",
    "reason": "User requested removal via support ticket #4521"
  }'
```

### Example Response

```json
{
  "id": "sup_i9j0k1l2",
  "email": "optout@example.com",
  "source": "manual",
  "reason": "User requested removal via support ticket #4521",
  "groupId": null,
  "createdAt": "2026-03-19T14:30:00.000Z"
}
```

***

## Bulk Add Suppressions

Suppress multiple addresses in a single request.

```
POST /suppressions/bulk
```

### Request Body

| Field     | Type      | Required | Description                              |
| --------- | --------- | -------- | ---------------------------------------- |
| `emails`  | string\[] | Yes      | Email addresses to suppress (max 10,000) |
| `reason`  | string    | No       | Shared reason for all suppressions       |
| `groupId` | string    | No       | Suppression group ID                     |

### Example Request

```bash
curl -X POST https://app.northrelay.ca/api/v1/suppressions/bulk \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer nr_live_xxxxxxxxxxxx" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "emails": [
      "bounce1@example.com",
      "bounce2@example.com",
      "bounce3@example.com"
    ],
    "reason": "Imported from previous ESP suppression list"
  }'
```

### Example Response

```json
{
  "added": 3,
  "duplicates": 0,
  "total": 3
}
```

Addresses that are already suppressed are counted as `duplicates` and not duplicated in the list.

***

## Remove Suppression

Remove a single suppression, allowing future emails to that address.

```
DELETE /suppressions/{id}
```

### Example Request

```bash
curl -X DELETE https://app.northrelay.ca/api/v1/suppressions/sup_a1b2c3d4 \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer nr_live_xxxxxxxxxxxx"
```

### Example Response

```json
{
  "id": "sup_a1b2c3d4",
  "deleted": true
}
```

Use caution when removing bounce or complaint suppressions. Sending to addresses that previously bounced or complained can damage your sender reputation.

***

## Suppression Groups

Suppression groups enable category-based unsubscribes. Instead of globally suppressing an address, you can suppress it only for specific email categories (e.g., "marketing" but not "transactional").

### List Suppression Groups

```
GET /suppressions/groups
```

### Example Response

```json
{
  "data": [
    {
      "id": "grp_m1n2o3p4",
      "name": "Marketing",
      "description": "Promotional emails and newsletters",
      "suppressionCount": 89,
      "createdAt": "2026-02-01T10:00:00.000Z"
    },
    {
      "id": "grp_q5r6s7t8",
      "name": "Product Updates",
      "description": "Feature announcements and changelog",
      "suppressionCount": 12,
      "createdAt": "2026-02-01T10:00:00.000Z"
    }
  ]
}
```

### Create Suppression Group

```
POST /suppressions/groups
```

| Field         | Type   | Required | Description       |
| ------------- | ------ | -------- | ----------------- |
| `name`        | string | Yes      | Group name        |
| `description` | string | No       | Group description |

### Delete Suppression Group

```
DELETE /suppressions/groups/{id}
```

Deleting a group also removes all suppressions in that group. Those addresses become eligible for delivery again.

### Using Groups When Sending

Include a `categories` array in your [Send Email](/api-reference/sending#send-email) request. NorthRelay checks the recipient against suppression groups that match those categories:

```json
{
  "from": { "email": "hello@yourdomain.com" },
  "to": [{ "email": "alice@example.com" }],
  "content": {
    "subject": "New features in March",
    "html": "<p>Check out what's new...</p>"
  },
  "categories": ["product-updates"]
}
```

If `alice@example.com` is suppressed in the "Product Updates" group, the email is dropped. If she is only suppressed in the "Marketing" group, the email is delivered.

***

## Suppression Object Reference

| Field       | Type           | Description                                            |
| ----------- | -------------- | ------------------------------------------------------ |
| `id`        | string         | Unique suppression identifier                          |
| `email`     | string         | Suppressed email address                               |
| `source`    | string         | `bounce`, `complaint`, `manual`, or `unsubscribe`      |
| `reason`    | string \| null | Reason for suppression                                 |
| `groupId`   | string \| null | Suppression group ID, or `null` for global suppression |
| `createdAt` | string         | ISO 8601 creation timestamp                            |


# Templates API



# Templates API

Templates let you define reusable email layouts with dynamic variables. Use Handlebars-style `{{variable}}` syntax for personalization. Templates can be referenced by ID when sending emails, keeping your sending code clean and your email content centrally managed.

## List Templates

Retrieve all templates for your account.

```
GET /templates
```

### Query Parameters

| Parameter | Type    | Default | Description              |
| --------- | ------- | ------- | ------------------------ |
| `page`    | integer | `1`     | Page number              |
| `limit`   | integer | `25`    | Items per page (max 100) |
| `search`  | string  | --      | Search by template name  |

### Example Request

```bash
curl https://app.northrelay.ca/api/v1/templates \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer nr_live_xxxxxxxxxxxx"
```

### Example Response

```json
{
  "data": [
    {
      "id": "tmpl_a1b2c3d4",
      "name": "Welcome Email",
      "subject": "Welcome to {{company_name}}, {{first_name}}!",
      "html": "<h1>Welcome, {{first_name}}</h1><p>Thanks for joining {{company_name}}.</p>",
      "text": "Welcome, {{first_name}}! Thanks for joining {{company_name}}.",
      "variables": ["first_name", "company_name"],
      "themeId": "theme_x1y2z3",
      "createdAt": "2026-03-01T10:00:00.000Z",
      "updatedAt": "2026-03-15T08:00:00.000Z"
    }
  ],
  "pagination": {
    "page": 1,
    "limit": 25,
    "total": 12,
    "totalPages": 1
  }
}
```

***

## Get Template

Retrieve a single template by ID.

```
GET /templates/{id}
```

### Example Request

```bash
curl https://app.northrelay.ca/api/v1/templates/tmpl_a1b2c3d4 \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer nr_live_xxxxxxxxxxxx"
```

***

## Create Template

Create a new email template.

```
POST /templates
```

### Request Body

| Field     | Type   | Required    | Description                                               |
| --------- | ------ | ----------- | --------------------------------------------------------- |
| `name`    | string | Yes         | Template name (for internal reference)                    |
| `subject` | string | Yes         | Subject line; supports `{{variable}}` syntax              |
| `html`    | string | Conditional | HTML body; at least one of `html` or `text` is required   |
| `text`    | string | Conditional | Plain-text body                                           |
| `themeId` | string | No          | Theme ID for consistent branding (header, footer, colors) |

### Example Request

```bash
curl -X POST https://app.northrelay.ca/api/v1/templates \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer nr_live_xxxxxxxxxxxx" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "name": "Order Confirmation",
    "subject": "Order #{{order_id}} confirmed",
    "html": "<h1>Thank you, {{first_name}}!</h1><p>Your order #{{order_id}} has been confirmed.</p><p>Total: {{total}}</p>",
    "text": "Thank you, {{first_name}}! Your order #{{order_id}} has been confirmed. Total: {{total}}"
  }'
```

### Example Response

```json
{
  "id": "tmpl_e5f6g7h8",
  "name": "Order Confirmation",
  "subject": "Order #{{order_id}} confirmed",
  "html": "<h1>Thank you, {{first_name}}!</h1><p>Your order #{{order_id}} has been confirmed.</p><p>Total: {{total}}</p>",
  "text": "Thank you, {{first_name}}! Your order #{{order_id}} has been confirmed. Total: {{total}}",
  "variables": ["order_id", "first_name", "total"],
  "themeId": null,
  "createdAt": "2026-03-19T14:30:00.000Z",
  "updatedAt": "2026-03-19T14:30:00.000Z"
}
```

The `variables` array is automatically extracted from `{{variable}}` placeholders in the subject, html, and text fields.

***

## Update Template

Update an existing template. Only the fields you include are changed.

```
PUT /templates/{id}
```

### Request Body

| Field     | Type           | Required | Description                             |
| --------- | -------------- | -------- | --------------------------------------- |
| `name`    | string         | No       | Updated template name                   |
| `subject` | string         | No       | Updated subject line                    |
| `html`    | string         | No       | Updated HTML body                       |
| `text`    | string         | No       | Updated plain-text body                 |
| `themeId` | string \| null | No       | Updated theme ID; pass `null` to remove |

### Example Request

```bash
curl -X PUT https://app.northrelay.ca/api/v1/templates/tmpl_e5f6g7h8 \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer nr_live_xxxxxxxxxxxx" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "subject": "Order #{{order_id}} confirmed - {{company_name}}",
    "themeId": "theme_x1y2z3"
  }'
```

***

## Delete Template

Permanently delete a template. Emails that reference this template by ID will fail to send after deletion.

```
DELETE /templates/{id}
```

### Example Request

```bash
curl -X DELETE https://app.northrelay.ca/api/v1/templates/tmpl_e5f6g7h8 \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer nr_live_xxxxxxxxxxxx"
```

### Example Response

```json
{
  "id": "tmpl_e5f6g7h8",
  "deleted": true
}
```

***

## Preview / Render Template

Render a template with sample variables without sending an email. Useful for previewing in your application or generating HTML for approval workflows.

```
POST /templates/{id}/render
```

### Request Body

| Field       | Type                    | Required | Description                   |
| ----------- | ----------------------- | -------- | ----------------------------- |
| `variables` | Record\<string, string> | No       | Variable values to substitute |

### Example Request

```bash
curl -X POST https://app.northrelay.ca/api/v1/templates/tmpl_e5f6g7h8/render \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer nr_live_xxxxxxxxxxxx" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "variables": {
      "first_name": "Alice",
      "order_id": "1234",
      "total": "$49.99"
    }
  }'
```

### Example Response

```json
{
  "subject": "Order #1234 confirmed",
  "html": "<h1>Thank you, Alice!</h1><p>Your order #1234 has been confirmed.</p><p>Total: $49.99</p>",
  "text": "Thank you, Alice! Your order #1234 has been confirmed. Total: $49.99"
}
```

Unresolved variables remain as literal `{{variable_name}}` in the output.

***

## Test Send

Send a rendered template to a test address. This counts against your monthly quota.

```
POST /templates/{id}/test
```

### Request Body

| Field       | Type                                                | Required | Description                          |
| ----------- | --------------------------------------------------- | -------- | ------------------------------------ |
| `to`        | string                                              | Yes      | Recipient email address for the test |
| `from`      | [EmailAddress](/api-reference/sending#emailaddress) | Yes      | Sender address                       |
| `variables` | Record\<string, string>                             | No       | Variable values to substitute        |

### Example Request

```bash
curl -X POST https://app.northrelay.ca/api/v1/templates/tmpl_e5f6g7h8/test \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer nr_live_xxxxxxxxxxxx" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "to": "test@yourdomain.com",
    "from": { "email": "hello@yourdomain.com", "name": "Acme Inc" },
    "variables": {
      "first_name": "Test User",
      "order_id": "0000",
      "total": "$0.00"
    }
  }'
```

### Example Response

```json
{
  "id": "msg_t1e2s3t4",
  "status": "queued",
  "templateId": "tmpl_e5f6g7h8"
}
```

***

## Using Templates When Sending

Reference a template by its `templateId` in the [Send Email](/api-reference/sending#send-email) or [Batch Send](/api-reference/sending#batch-send) content object:

```json
{
  "from": { "email": "hello@yourdomain.com" },
  "to": [{ "email": "alice@example.com" }],
  "content": {
    "templateId": "tmpl_e5f6g7h8"
  },
  "variables": {
    "first_name": "Alice",
    "order_id": "1234",
    "total": "$49.99"
  }
}
```

When `templateId` is provided, the template's subject, HTML, and text are used. The `subject` field in the content object is optional and overrides the template subject if provided.

## Template Variables

Variables use Handlebars-style double-brace syntax: `{{variable_name}}`.

* Variable names are case-sensitive
* Unresolved variables are left as-is in the rendered output
* Variables work in the subject line, HTML body, and plain-text body
* In batch sends, per-recipient `variables` override top-level `variables`

### Template Object Reference

| Field       | Type           | Description                                |
| ----------- | -------------- | ------------------------------------------ |
| `id`        | string         | Unique template identifier                 |
| `name`      | string         | Internal template name                     |
| `subject`   | string         | Subject line with optional `{{variables}}` |
| `html`      | string \| null | HTML body                                  |
| `text`      | string \| null | Plain-text body                            |
| `variables` | string\[]      | Auto-extracted variable names              |
| `themeId`   | string \| null | Associated theme ID                        |
| `createdAt` | string         | ISO 8601 creation timestamp                |
| `updatedAt` | string         | ISO 8601 last update timestamp             |


# Webhooks



# Webhooks

Webhooks deliver real-time HTTP POST notifications to your server when email events occur (delivery, bounce, open, click, etc.). Use webhooks to update your application state, trigger workflows, or monitor deliverability.

**Tier requirement:** Webhooks are available on Startup ($29/mo) and above.

## Event Types

| Event              | Description                                          | Trigger                  |
| ------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------ |
| `email.delivered`  | Email accepted by the recipient's mail server        | Successful SMTP delivery |
| `email.bounced`    | Email permanently rejected (hard bounce)             | 5xx SMTP response        |
| `email.deferred`   | Email temporarily rejected (soft bounce); will retry | 4xx SMTP response        |
| `email.opened`     | Recipient opened the email                           | Tracking pixel loaded    |
| `email.clicked`    | Recipient clicked a link                             | Tracked link followed    |
| `email.complained` | Recipient marked the email as spam                   | Feedback loop report     |

***

## List Webhooks

Retrieve all configured webhook endpoints.

```
GET /webhooks
```

### Example Request

```bash
curl https://app.northrelay.ca/api/v1/webhooks \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer nr_live_xxxxxxxxxxxx"
```

### Example Response

```json
{
  "data": [
    {
      "id": "wh_a1b2c3d4",
      "url": "https://yourapp.com/webhooks/northrelay",
      "events": ["email.delivered", "email.bounced", "email.complained"],
      "active": true,
      "secret": "whsec_xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx",
      "createdAt": "2026-03-01T10:00:00.000Z",
      "updatedAt": "2026-03-01T10:00:00.000Z"
    }
  ],
  "pagination": {
    "page": 1,
    "limit": 25,
    "total": 1,
    "totalPages": 1
  }
}
```

***

## Create Webhook

Register a new webhook endpoint.

```
POST /webhooks
```

### Request Body

| Field    | Type      | Required | Description                                        |
| -------- | --------- | -------- | -------------------------------------------------- |
| `url`    | string    | Yes      | HTTPS endpoint URL that will receive POST requests |
| `events` | string\[] | Yes      | Array of event types to subscribe to               |
| `active` | boolean   | No       | Whether the webhook is active (default: `true`)    |

### Example Request

```bash
curl -X POST https://app.northrelay.ca/api/v1/webhooks \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer nr_live_xxxxxxxxxxxx" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "url": "https://yourapp.com/webhooks/northrelay",
    "events": ["email.delivered", "email.bounced", "email.complained"]
  }'
```

### Example Response

```json
{
  "id": "wh_a1b2c3d4",
  "url": "https://yourapp.com/webhooks/northrelay",
  "events": ["email.delivered", "email.bounced", "email.complained"],
  "active": true,
  "secret": "whsec_xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx",
  "createdAt": "2026-03-19T14:30:00.000Z"
}
```

Save the `secret` value -- you will need it to verify webhook signatures. The secret is only returned in the create response.

***

## Update Webhook

Update an existing webhook endpoint.

```
PUT /webhooks/{id}
```

### Request Body

| Field    | Type      | Required | Description                   |
| -------- | --------- | -------- | ----------------------------- |
| `url`    | string    | No       | New endpoint URL              |
| `events` | string\[] | No       | Updated event subscriptions   |
| `active` | boolean   | No       | Enable or disable the webhook |

### Example Request

```bash
curl -X PUT https://app.northrelay.ca/api/v1/webhooks/wh_a1b2c3d4 \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer nr_live_xxxxxxxxxxxx" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "events": ["email.delivered", "email.bounced", "email.opened", "email.clicked", "email.complained"],
    "active": true
  }'
```

***

## Delete Webhook

Remove a webhook endpoint. No further events will be delivered.

```
DELETE /webhooks/{id}
```

### Example Request

```bash
curl -X DELETE https://app.northrelay.ca/api/v1/webhooks/wh_a1b2c3d4 \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer nr_live_xxxxxxxxxxxx"
```

### Example Response

```json
{
  "id": "wh_a1b2c3d4",
  "deleted": true
}
```

***

## Payload Format

Webhook POST requests are sent as JSON with the following structure:

```json
{
  "id": "evt_x1y2z3",
  "type": "email.delivered",
  "timestamp": "2026-03-19T14:35:00.000Z",
  "data": {
    "messageId": "msg_a1b2c3d4e5f6",
    "from": "hello@yourdomain.com",
    "to": "alice@example.com",
    "subject": "Your order has shipped",
    "categories": ["transactional", "order-updates"],
    "tags": { "order_id": "1234" },
    "metadata": { "internal_ref": "ship-batch-42" }
  }
}
```

### Event-Specific Data Fields

**email.delivered**

| Field          | Type   | Description                           |
| -------------- | ------ | ------------------------------------- |
| `messageId`    | string | NorthRelay message ID                 |
| `from`         | string | Sender address                        |
| `to`           | string | Recipient address                     |
| `subject`      | string | Email subject line                    |
| `deliveredAt`  | string | ISO 8601 delivery timestamp           |
| `smtpResponse` | string | SMTP server response (e.g., `250 OK`) |

**email.bounced**

| Field           | Type   | Description                          |
| --------------- | ------ | ------------------------------------ |
| `messageId`     | string | NorthRelay message ID                |
| `from`          | string | Sender address                       |
| `to`            | string | Recipient address                    |
| `bounceType`    | string | `hard` or `soft`                     |
| `bounceCode`    | string | SMTP error code (e.g., `550`)        |
| `bounceMessage` | string | Bounce reason from the remote server |

**email.opened**

| Field       | Type   | Description              |
| ----------- | ------ | ------------------------ |
| `messageId` | string | NorthRelay message ID    |
| `to`        | string | Recipient address        |
| `openedAt`  | string | ISO 8601 open timestamp  |
| `userAgent` | string | Recipient's email client |
| `ipAddress` | string | Recipient's IP address   |

**email.clicked**

| Field       | Type   | Description                |
| ----------- | ------ | -------------------------- |
| `messageId` | string | NorthRelay message ID      |
| `to`        | string | Recipient address          |
| `clickedAt` | string | ISO 8601 click timestamp   |
| `url`       | string | Link that was clicked      |
| `userAgent` | string | Recipient's browser/client |

**email.complained**

| Field          | Type   | Description                        |
| -------------- | ------ | ---------------------------------- |
| `messageId`    | string | NorthRelay message ID              |
| `to`           | string | Recipient address                  |
| `complainedAt` | string | ISO 8601 complaint timestamp       |
| `feedbackType` | string | Feedback loop type (e.g., `abuse`) |

**email.deferred**

| Field          | Type    | Description                            |
| -------------- | ------- | -------------------------------------- |
| `messageId`    | string  | NorthRelay message ID                  |
| `to`           | string  | Recipient address                      |
| `deferredAt`   | string  | ISO 8601 deferral timestamp            |
| `smtpResponse` | string  | Temporary failure response             |
| `retryCount`   | integer | Number of delivery attempts so far     |
| `nextRetryAt`  | string  | ISO 8601 time of next delivery attempt |

***

## Signature Verification

Every webhook request includes an `X-NorthRelay-Signature` header containing an HMAC-SHA256 signature computed from the raw request body and your webhook secret.

**Always verify signatures** to ensure requests originate from NorthRelay and have not been tampered with.

### Verification Algorithm

1. Extract the `X-NorthRelay-Signature` header value
2. Compute HMAC-SHA256 of the raw request body using your webhook `secret` as the key
3. Compare the computed signature with the header value using a timing-safe comparison

### Node.js Example

```typescript
import crypto from "crypto";

function verifyWebhookSignature(
  body: string,
  signature: string,
  secret: string
): boolean {
  const computed = crypto
    .createHmac("sha256", secret)
    .update(body, "utf8")
    .digest("hex");

  return crypto.timingSafeEqual(
    Buffer.from(computed, "hex"),
    Buffer.from(signature, "hex")
  );
}

// In your webhook handler:
app.post("/webhooks/northrelay", (req, res) => {
  const signature = req.headers["x-northrelay-signature"] as string;
  const rawBody = req.body; // must be the raw string, not parsed JSON

  if (!verifyWebhookSignature(rawBody, signature, process.env.WEBHOOK_SECRET!)) {
    return res.status(401).json({ error: "Invalid signature" });
  }

  const event = JSON.parse(rawBody);
  // Process event...
  res.status(200).json({ received: true });
});
```

### Python Example

```python
import hmac
import hashlib

def verify_webhook_signature(body: bytes, signature: str, secret: str) -> bool:
    computed = hmac.new(
        secret.encode("utf-8"),
        body,
        hashlib.sha256
    ).hexdigest()
    return hmac.compare_digest(computed, signature)

# In your webhook handler (Flask):
@app.route("/webhooks/northrelay", methods=["POST"])
def handle_webhook():
    signature = request.headers.get("X-NorthRelay-Signature")
    if not verify_webhook_signature(request.data, signature, WEBHOOK_SECRET):
        return {"error": "Invalid signature"}, 401

    event = request.get_json()
    # Process event...
    return {"received": True}, 200
```

***

## Retry Policy

If your endpoint does not respond with a `2xx` status code within 10 seconds, NorthRelay retries the delivery:

| Attempt   | Delay      |
| --------- | ---------- |
| 1st retry | 30 seconds |
| 2nd retry | 2 minutes  |
| 3rd retry | 15 minutes |
| 4th retry | 1 hour     |
| 5th retry | 4 hours    |

After 5 failed retries, the event is marked as failed. Failed events are visible in **Dashboard > Webhooks > Event Log** for 30 days.

### Best Practices

1. **Respond quickly.** Return `200` immediately and process the event asynchronously. Do not perform heavy work in the request handler.
2. **Handle duplicates.** Use the event `id` to deduplicate. The same event may be delivered more than once.
3. **Verify signatures.** Always validate the `X-NorthRelay-Signature` header.
4. **Use HTTPS.** Webhook URLs must use HTTPS. HTTP endpoints are rejected.
5. **Log failures.** Monitor your webhook endpoint health in the dashboard.


# Billing FAQ



# Billing FAQ

## Plan Changes

### Can I switch plans at any time?

Yes. You can upgrade or downgrade your plan at any time from **Dashboard > Billing > Change Plan**.

* **Upgrades** take effect immediately. You get instant access to the new tier's features and sending limits.
* **Downgrades** take effect at the end of your current billing cycle. You keep your current features until then.

### Is my billing prorated when I upgrade?

Yes. When you upgrade mid-cycle, you are charged the prorated difference for the remaining days in your billing period. For example, if you upgrade from Micro ($5/mo) to Startup ($29/mo) halfway through your cycle, you pay approximately $12 for the remaining half-month.

### Is my billing prorated when I downgrade?

No. Downgrades take effect at the end of your current billing cycle. You are not refunded for the remaining days on your current plan, but you retain full access to your current tier's features until the cycle ends.

### What happens to my custom domains if I downgrade below Startup?

Custom domains are a Startup+ feature. If you downgrade to Micro or Sandbox:

* Your custom domains are **deactivated** (not deleted) at the end of the billing cycle
* Emails will send from your NorthRelay system subdomain instead
* Your domain configuration is preserved -- if you upgrade again, your domains reactivate without needing to re-verify DNS

### What happens to my webhooks if I downgrade below Startup?

Webhooks are a Startup+ feature. If you downgrade:

* Webhook endpoints are **paused** at the end of the billing cycle
* No events are delivered while paused
* Webhook configurations are preserved -- upgrading reactivates them

### What happens to my team members if I downgrade below Scale?

Team members are a Scale+ feature. If you downgrade:

* Team member access is **revoked** at the end of the billing cycle
* Team member accounts are preserved -- upgrading restores their access

## Quotas & Limits

### What happens if I exceed my monthly email quota?

When you reach your monthly sending limit:

* Additional send requests return an error: `451 4.7.1 Rate limit exceeded`
* The API returns a `QuotaExceededError` with a clear message
* Already-queued and in-progress emails continue to deliver
* Your dashboard shows a warning when you reach 80% of your quota
* Your quota resets at the start of your next billing cycle

You can upgrade your plan at any time to get a higher quota immediately.

### Do unused emails roll over to the next month?

No. Email quotas reset at the start of each billing cycle. Unused emails do not carry over.

### Is there an overage option instead of upgrading?

Not currently. If you regularly exceed your quota, upgrading to the next tier is the recommended approach. Enterprise plans can include custom quota arrangements -- [contact sales](mailto:sales@northrelay.ca).

## Payments

### What payment methods do you accept?

NorthRelay accepts Visa, Mastercard, and American Express credit and debit cards. All payments are processed securely through Stripe.

### What currency are charges in?

All charges are in **USD** (US Dollars).

### When am I charged?

You are charged on the same day each month, based on the date you first subscribed. For example, if you signed up on March 15, you are charged on the 15th of each month.

### What if my payment fails?

If a payment fails:

1. You receive an email notification
2. Stripe automatically retries after 3 days, then again after 5 days
3. If all retries fail, your account is downgraded to the Sandbox (free) tier
4. You can resolve it at any time by updating your payment method and retrying

No data is lost during a payment failure. Your account, domains, templates, and contacts are all preserved.

### Can I pay annually?

Annual billing is not currently available for self-service plans. Enterprise customers can arrange annual billing -- [contact sales](mailto:sales@northrelay.ca).

## Refunds

### What is your refund policy?

NorthRelay offers a **14-day money-back guarantee** on your first payment for any paid plan. If you are not satisfied within 14 days of your first charge, contact support for a full refund.

After the 14-day window:

* Monthly charges are **not refundable** once the billing cycle has started
* Downgrades take effect at the end of the cycle (no partial refund)
* If there is a service issue on NorthRelay's end (outage, billing error), refunds or credits are handled on a case-by-case basis

### How do I request a refund?

Email [support@northrelay.ca](mailto:support@northrelay.ca) with your account email and the reason for your request. Refund requests within the 14-day window are processed within 5-10 business days.

## Free Tier

### What are the Sandbox tier limitations?

The Sandbox (free) tier includes:

* 3,000 emails per month
* Shared IP pool (shared with other free and Micro tier senders)
* Sends from a NorthRelay system subdomain only (no custom domains)
* 100 MB inbox storage
* 5 templates, 1 contact list
* No webhooks or team features

There is no time limit on the Sandbox tier. You can use it indefinitely.

### Do I need a credit card for the free tier?

No. The Sandbox tier requires no credit card and no payment information.

## Enterprise

### How does Enterprise pricing work?

Enterprise pricing is custom based on your requirements:

* **Volume:** Monthly sending volume above 200,000 emails
* **Infrastructure:** Dedicated IPs, custom HELO hostnames
* **Support:** Priority support with guaranteed response times, dedicated account manager
* **Compliance:** Custom SLA, data processing agreements, security reviews

[Contact sales](mailto:sales@northrelay.ca) for a quote.

### Can I get a dedicated IP on a non-Enterprise plan?

Dedicated IPs are only available on the Enterprise tier. Startup and Scale tiers use the isolated pool, which provides strong reputation protection without a dedicated IP. For most senders, the isolated pool delivers equivalent results.

### Do you offer non-profit or startup discounts?

Contact [sales@northrelay.ca](mailto:sales@northrelay.ca) to discuss special pricing for qualified non-profits and early-stage startups.

## Related

* [Plans & Pricing](/docs/billing/plans) for detailed tier comparison
* [Invoices](/docs/billing/invoices) for payment and receipt management


# Invoices



# Invoices

NorthRelay uses Stripe for all billing and payment processing. Your invoices, payment methods, and billing history are managed through the dashboard.

## Accessing Invoices

1. Go to **Dashboard > Billing**
2. Click the **Invoices** tab
3. Your invoice history is displayed in reverse chronological order

Each row shows:

* Invoice date
* Invoice number
* Amount
* Status (Paid, Pending, Failed)
* A link to view or download

## Invoice Details

Each invoice includes:

| Field              | Description                                               |
| ------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Invoice Number** | Unique identifier (e.g., `INV-2026-0042`)                 |
| **Date**           | The billing date                                          |
| **Due Date**       | When payment is due (typically the same day for auto-pay) |
| **Plan**           | Your current tier and billing period                      |
| **Amount**         | The charge for the billing period                         |
| **Tax**            | Applicable taxes (if any, based on your billing address)  |
| **Total**          | Final amount charged                                      |
| **Status**         | Paid, Pending, or Failed                                  |
| **Payment Method** | The card or method used for payment                       |

## Downloading Receipts

To download a PDF receipt:

1. Go to **Dashboard > Billing > Invoices**
2. Click **Download** on the invoice row
3. A PDF receipt downloads to your device

Receipts include all information needed for expense reporting and accounting: invoice number, date, line items, tax breakdown, and payment confirmation.

## Payment Methods

NorthRelay accepts:

* **Credit cards** -- Visa, Mastercard, American Express
* **Debit cards** -- Visa Debit, Mastercard Debit

All payment processing is handled securely by Stripe. NorthRelay never stores your full card number.

### Adding a Payment Method

1. Go to **Dashboard > Billing**
2. Click **Payment Methods**
3. Click **Add Payment Method**
4. Enter your card details
5. Click **Save**

### Updating Your Default Payment Method

1. Go to **Dashboard > Billing > Payment Methods**
2. Click **Set as Default** on the card you want to use
3. Future charges will use this card

### Removing a Payment Method

1. Go to **Dashboard > Billing > Payment Methods**
2. Click **Remove** on the card you want to delete
3. Confirm removal

You cannot remove your only payment method while on a paid plan. Add a new method first, then remove the old one.

## Updating Billing Information

To update your billing name, address, or email (for invoice delivery):

1. Go to **Dashboard > Billing**
2. Click **Billing Details**
3. Update your information
4. Click **Save**

Changes apply to future invoices. Past invoices are not retroactively updated.

## Failed Payments

If a payment fails:

1. NorthRelay sends an email notification to your account email
2. The invoice status shows as **Failed** in your dashboard
3. Stripe automatically retries the charge after 3 days, then again after 5 days
4. If all retries fail, your account may be downgraded to the Sandbox tier

To resolve a failed payment:

1. Go to **Dashboard > Billing > Payment Methods**
2. Update your payment method or add a new card
3. Click **Retry Payment** on the failed invoice
4. Your service is restored immediately upon successful payment

## Billing Cycle

* Billing is monthly, charged on the same day each month (based on your signup date)
* If you sign up on the 31st, you are charged on the last day of shorter months
* All amounts are in **USD**
* Invoices are generated at the start of each billing cycle

## Related

* [Plans & Pricing](/docs/billing/plans) for tier comparison
* [Billing FAQ](/docs/billing/faq) for common questions about billing


# Plans & Pricing



# Plans & Pricing

NorthRelay offers five tiers designed to match your sending volume and feature needs. All paid plans include a 14-day free trial.

## Plan Comparison

| Feature              | Sandbox | Micro  | Startup   | Scale     | Enterprise |
| -------------------- | ------- | ------ | --------- | --------- | ---------- |
| **Price**            | Free    | $5/mo  | $29/mo    | $65/mo    | Custom     |
| **Emails/Month**     | 3,000   | 10,000 | 50,000    | 150,000   | 200,000+   |
| **IP Pool**          | Shared  | Shared | Isolated  | Isolated  | Dedicated  |
| **Custom Domains**   | --      | --     | Yes       | Yes       | Yes        |
| **Inbox Access**     | Yes     | Yes    | Yes       | Yes       | Yes        |
| **Inbox Storage**    | 100 MB  | 500 MB | 5 GB      | 25 GB     | 50 GB      |
| **Webhooks**         | --      | --     | Yes       | Yes       | Yes        |
| **Team Members**     | --      | --     | --        | Yes       | Yes        |
| **API Access**       | Yes     | Yes    | Yes       | Yes       | Yes        |
| **Templates**        | 5       | 25     | Unlimited | Unlimited | Unlimited  |
| **Contact Lists**    | 1       | 5      | Unlimited | Unlimited | Unlimited  |
| **Priority Support** | --      | --     | --        | --        | Yes        |
| **Dedicated IPs**    | --      | --     | --        | --        | Yes        |
| **Custom SLA**       | --      | --     | --        | --        | Yes        |

## Sandbox (Free)

The Sandbox tier is for testing and evaluation. No credit card required.

* **3,000 emails per month** -- enough to test your integration
* **Shared IP pool** -- your emails share an IP with other free-tier senders
* Sends from a NorthRelay system subdomain (`yourname.north-pipe.ca`)
* Basic inbox access with 100 MB storage
* Full API access with 5 templates and 1 contact list
* No custom domains, webhooks, or team features

**Best for:** Developers evaluating NorthRelay, personal projects, proof-of-concept integrations.

## Micro ($5/month)

The Micro tier provides a cleaner sending environment at a low cost. It acts as the gateway tier -- better than free, with a clear upgrade path.

* **10,000 emails per month**
* **Shared IP pool** -- same pool as Sandbox but with better rate limits
* Sends from a NorthRelay system subdomain (no custom domains)
* Inbox access with 500 MB storage
* 25 templates and 5 contact lists
* No custom domains, webhooks, or team features

**Best for:** Small projects, low-volume transactional email, developers who need more than the free tier.

## Startup ($29/month)

The Startup tier is the first tier with full feature access. It unlocks custom domains and the isolated IP pool, which is the core NorthRelay differentiator.

* **50,000 emails per month**
* **Isolated IP pool** -- your emails route through a physically separate IP, protected from other senders' reputation
* **Custom domains** -- send from your own domain with full SPF/DKIM/DMARC alignment
* **Webhooks** -- receive real-time delivery notifications
* Inbox access with 5 GB storage
* Unlimited templates and contact lists
* Full diagnostic tools access

**Best for:** SaaS products, growing businesses, anyone who needs deliverability guarantees.

## Scale ($65/month)

The Scale tier adds higher volume and team collaboration.

* **150,000 emails per month**
* **Isolated IP pool** -- same protected infrastructure as Startup
* **Custom domains** with full authentication
* **Webhooks** for delivery tracking
* **Team members** -- invite colleagues with role-based access
* Inbox access with 25 GB storage
* Unlimited templates and contact lists
* Full diagnostic tools access

**Best for:** High-volume senders, marketing teams, multi-person operations.

## Enterprise (Custom Pricing)

The Enterprise tier provides dedicated infrastructure and white-glove support.

* **200,000+ emails per month** (custom limits)
* **Dedicated IP pool** -- your own IP addresses, not shared with any other sender
* **Dedicated IPs** -- one or more IPs assigned exclusively to your account
* **Custom domains** with full authentication
* **Webhooks** and **team members**
* Inbox access with 50 GB storage
* **Priority support** with guaranteed response times
* **Custom SLA** tailored to your requirements
* Custom onboarding and migration assistance

**Best for:** Large organizations, high-volume marketing platforms, businesses with strict compliance requirements.

[Contact sales](mailto:sales@northrelay.ca) for Enterprise pricing.

## Understanding IP Pools

NorthRelay's core differentiator is physical IP separation between tiers. This is the Two-Pool System.

### Shared Pool (Sandbox + Micro)

* Emails route through a shared public IP
* IP reputation is influenced by all senders on the pool
* Vulnerable to the "noisy neighbor" problem -- if another sender on the pool behaves badly, it can affect your deliverability
* HELO hostname: `shared.north-pipe.ca`

### Isolated Pool (Startup + Scale)

* Emails route through a separate, dedicated IP via WireGuard tunnel
* IP reputation is isolated from the shared pool
* Protected from noisy neighbors
* Consistently higher deliverability
* HELO hostname: `sovereign.north-pipe.ca`

### Dedicated Pool (Enterprise)

* Your own IP addresses, used only by your account
* Complete control over IP reputation
* Custom HELO hostname available

For a deeper explanation, see the [Two-Pool System](/docs/features/two-pool-system) documentation.

## Feature Gates

Some features are gated by tier to ensure quality of service:

| Feature          | Minimum Tier  | Why                                                     |
| ---------------- | ------------- | ------------------------------------------------------- |
| Custom domains   | Startup ($29) | Requires isolated IP pool for proper DKIM/SPF alignment |
| Webhooks         | Startup ($29) | Event delivery infrastructure has per-account overhead  |
| Team members     | Scale ($65)   | Multi-user access control and audit logging             |
| Dedicated IPs    | Enterprise    | Physical IP allocation and management                   |
| Priority support | Enterprise    | Dedicated support staff assignment                      |

## How to Upgrade

1. Go to **Dashboard > Billing**
2. Click **Change Plan**
3. Select your new tier
4. Enter payment information (if not already on file)
5. Confirm the upgrade

Upgrades take effect immediately. You get instant access to the new tier's features and sending limits. See the [Billing FAQ](/docs/billing/faq) for proration details.

## How to Downgrade

1. Go to **Dashboard > Billing**
2. Click **Change Plan**
3. Select a lower tier
4. Confirm the downgrade

Downgrades take effect at the end of your current billing cycle. You keep your current tier's features until then. Note that downgrading may disable features (e.g., custom domains, webhooks) that are not available on the lower tier.

## Related

* [Two-Pool System](/docs/features/two-pool-system) for IP pool details
* [Domain Verification](/docs/features/domain-verification) for custom domain setup (Startup+)
* [Billing FAQ](/docs/billing/faq) for common billing questions
* [Invoices](/docs/billing/invoices) for payment and receipt management


# Diagnostic Tools



# Diagnostic Tools

NorthRelay provides three built-in diagnostic tools to help you identify and resolve delivery issues without contacting support. Access them from **Dashboard > Tools**.

## Pre-Flight Check

The Pre-Flight Check validates your sending configuration before you send a single email. It tests DNS records, SMTP connectivity, and authentication in one pass.

### What It Tests

| Check                 | Description                                                              | Pass Criteria                                    |
| --------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------ |
| **SPF Record**        | Verifies your domain has a valid SPF TXT record that includes NorthRelay | Record contains `include:_spf.north-pipe.ca`     |
| **DKIM Record**       | Verifies the DKIM CNAME record points to NorthRelay's signing key        | CNAME resolves to the expected NorthRelay target |
| **DMARC Record**      | Checks for a DMARC policy on your domain                                 | `_dmarc.yourdomain.com` TXT record exists        |
| **MX Records**        | Validates your domain's MX configuration (for inbox domains)             | MX records resolve correctly                     |
| **SMTP Connectivity** | Tests that NorthRelay can establish an SMTP connection for your domain   | Connection succeeds within timeout               |
| **Authentication**    | Validates your API key and sending permissions                           | API key is valid and has send permission         |

### How to Use

1. Go to **Dashboard > Tools > Pre-Flight Check**
2. Enter the domain you want to test (must be added to your account)
3. Click **Run Check**
4. Review the results -- each check shows pass, fail, or warning with a specific message

### Interpreting Results

* **All green:** Your configuration is correct. You are ready to send.
* **Yellow warnings:** Non-critical issues (e.g., DMARC policy is `none` instead of `reject`). Sending will work but deliverability may not be optimal.
* **Red failures:** Critical issues that will prevent delivery or cause authentication failures. Fix these before sending.

Each failed check includes an **action** field explaining exactly what to fix and a link to the relevant documentation.

## Blocklist Monitor

The Blocklist Monitor checks your domain and sending IPs against 13 industry-standard DNS-based Real-time Blocklists (RBLs). Being listed on a blocklist can severely impact your deliverability.

### Provider Coverage

The monitor checks 13 consolidated providers organized into four tiers by importance:

#### Tier 1: Industry Standard (3 providers)

These are the blocklists that major mailbox providers actively check. A listing here has the most impact.

| Provider         | Type     | Description                                                                    |
| ---------------- | -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| **Spamhaus ZEN** | IP-based | Combined Spamhaus blocklist (SBL + XBL + PBL). The most widely used blocklist. |
| **Barracuda**    | IP-based | Barracuda Networks reputation system. Used by many enterprise mail gateways.   |
| **SpamCop**      | IP-based | Crowdsourced spam reporting. Listings are time-based and auto-expire.          |

#### Tier 2: Compromised Hosts & Network Threats (6 providers)

These detect compromised systems, botnets, and network-level abuse.

| Provider          | Type     | Description                                                          |
| ----------------- | -------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **CBL**           | IP-based | Composite Blocking List. Detects IPs sending spam from bots/malware. |
| **PSBL**          | IP-based | Passive Spam Block List. Trap-based detection.                       |
| **Invaluement**   | IP-based | Detects snowshoe spam and bulk senders with poor practices.          |
| **Backscatterer** | IP-based | Detects IPs generating backscatter (bounces to forged senders).      |
| **0spam**         | IP-based | Zero-spam project. Algorithmic detection.                            |
| **SpamRats**      | IP-based | Detects IPs on dynamic/residential ranges sending spam.              |

#### Tier 3: Reputation & Behavior Systems (3 providers)

These track sending reputation and behavioral patterns over time.

| Provider      | Type     | Description                                                |
| ------------- | -------- | ---------------------------------------------------------- |
| **HOSTKARMA** | IP-based | Junk Email Filter. Reputation-based scoring.               |
| **Mailspike** | IP-based | Real-time reputation tracking with historical data.        |
| **SORBS**     | IP-based | Spam and Open Relay Blocking System. Multiple zone checks. |

#### Tier 4: Domain & URI Reputation (1 provider)

Checks domain reputation rather than IP addresses.

| Provider        | Type         | Description                                                                         |
| --------------- | ------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **SURBL Multi** | Domain-based | Combined phishing, malware, and abuse domain list. Checks domains in email content. |

### How to Use

1. Go to **Dashboard > Tools > Blocklist Monitor**
2. Enter a domain or IP address to check
3. Click **Check**
4. Results appear grouped by tier in collapsible accordion sections

### Result Statuses

| Status           | Meaning                                       | Action                                           |
| ---------------- | --------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------ |
| **Clean**        | Not listed on this blocklist                  | No action needed                                 |
| **Listed**       | Your IP or domain is on this blocklist        | Follow the provider's delisting process          |
| **Warning**      | Listed on a lower-priority blocklist          | Monitor and consider delisting                   |
| **Rate Limited** | The blocklist provider rate-limited our query | Try again in a few minutes                       |
| **Offline**      | The blocklist provider is not responding      | Not your issue -- check again later              |
| **Timeout**      | Query timed out (3-second limit)              | Likely a provider issue -- retry                 |
| **Error**        | Unexpected error during lookup                | Retry; if persistent, contact NorthRelay support |

### If You Are Listed

1. **Identify the cause.** Review your [Reputation Monitoring](/docs/features/reputation-monitoring) dashboard for incidents.
2. **Fix the underlying issue.** High bounce rates and spam complaints are the most common causes of blocklisting.
3. **Request delisting.** Each blocklist provider has a delisting process:
   * **Spamhaus:** [https://check.spamhaus.org](https://check.spamhaus.org)
   * **Barracuda:** [https://www.barracudacentral.org/lookups](https://www.barracudacentral.org/lookups)
   * **SpamCop:** Listings auto-expire after reports stop (typically 24-48 hours)
4. **Re-check.** After delisting, run the Blocklist Monitor again to confirm.

### Technical Details

* All 13 providers are queried in parallel
* Each query has a 3-second timeout
* No registration or paid subscription required -- all providers are freely accessible
* Queries use standard DNS-based blocklist lookup (reverse IP + blocklist zone)

## Connectivity Doctor

The Connectivity Doctor tests the full SMTP delivery chain from NorthRelay to a destination mail server. Use it to diagnose why emails to a specific domain are failing.

### What It Tests

| Check               | Description                                      | What It Reveals                                              |
| ------------------- | ------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------ |
| **DNS Resolution**  | Resolves MX records for the destination domain   | Whether the destination domain has valid mail servers        |
| **SMTP Connection** | Connects to the destination MX server on port 25 | Whether the mail server is reachable                         |
| **TLS Handshake**   | Attempts STARTTLS upgrade                        | Whether the connection can be encrypted                      |
| **EHLO Response**   | Sends EHLO and checks server capabilities        | What extensions the server supports (SIZE, PIPELINING, etc.) |
| **Authentication**  | Tests that NorthRelay's sending IP is accepted   | Whether the destination server accepts mail from NorthRelay  |

### How to Use

1. Go to **Dashboard > Tools > Connectivity Doctor**
2. Enter the destination domain you are having trouble delivering to (e.g., `gmail.com`)
3. Click **Test Connection**
4. Review the step-by-step results

### Interpreting Results

Each step shows a pass/fail status with timing information:

* **DNS Resolution failed:** The destination domain has no MX records or its DNS is misconfigured. This is a problem on the recipient's side.
* **SMTP Connection failed:** The destination mail server is unreachable. Could be a firewall, server outage, or network issue.
* **TLS Handshake failed:** The destination server does not support STARTTLS or has a certificate issue. Emails may still deliver without encryption depending on your settings.
* **EHLO rejected:** The destination server rejected NorthRelay's HELO/EHLO. This could indicate IP-based blocking.
* **Authentication rejected:** The destination server is rejecting mail from NorthRelay's IP. Check the [Blocklist Monitor](#blocklist-monitor) to see if the IP is listed.

## When to Use Each Tool

| Scenario                                   | Recommended Tool                          |
| ------------------------------------------ | ----------------------------------------- |
| Setting up a new domain                    | Pre-Flight Check                          |
| Emails going to spam                       | Blocklist Monitor + Reputation Monitoring |
| Emails not delivering to a specific domain | Connectivity Doctor                       |
| Routine health check                       | Blocklist Monitor                         |
| Investigating a reputation drop            | All three tools                           |
| Before launching a new campaign            | Pre-Flight Check                          |

## Related

* [Domain Verification](/docs/features/domain-verification) for setting up DNS records
* [Reputation Monitoring](/docs/features/reputation-monitoring) for ongoing sender health
* [Two-Pool System](/docs/features/two-pool-system) for understanding IP isolation


# Domain Verification



# Domain Verification

Verifying your custom domain allows you to send email from your own addresses (e.g., `notifications@yourdomain.com`) instead of a NorthRelay system subdomain. Verified domains get proper SPF, DKIM, and DMARC alignment, which significantly improves deliverability and brand trust.

## Prerequisites

* **Startup tier or higher** ($29/mo) -- custom domains are not available on Sandbox or Micro plans
* Access to your domain's DNS management (e.g., Cloudflare, Route 53, GoDaddy)
* A domain you own and control

Sandbox and Micro tier users send from NorthRelay system subdomains on `north-pipe.ca`. To use your own domain, [upgrade to Startup or higher](/docs/billing/plans).

## Why Verify Your Domain?

| Benefit               | Without Verification            | With Verification             |
| --------------------- | ------------------------------- | ----------------------------- |
| **From address**      | `you@yourname.north-pipe.ca`    | `you@yourdomain.com`          |
| **DKIM signing**      | Signed with `north-pipe.ca` key | Signed with your domain's key |
| **SPF alignment**     | Partial (via include)           | Full alignment                |
| **DMARC compliance**  | Depends on receiver policy      | Full pass                     |
| **Brand recognition** | Generic NorthRelay subdomain    | Your brand in every inbox     |
| **Deliverability**    | Good                            | Best possible                 |

## Step-by-Step Verification

### Step 1: Add Your Domain

1. Go to **Dashboard > Domains**
2. Click **Add Domain**
3. Enter your domain hostname (e.g., `mail.yourdomain.com` or `yourdomain.com`)
4. Click **Add**

NorthRelay will generate the DNS records you need to add.

### Step 2: Get Your DNS Records

After adding the domain, the dashboard shows three DNS records to configure:

#### SPF Record (TXT)

Authorizes NorthRelay's mail servers to send on behalf of your domain.

| Field     | Value                                    |
| --------- | ---------------------------------------- |
| **Type**  | TXT                                      |
| **Host**  | `@` or `yourdomain.com`                  |
| **Value** | `v=spf1 include:_spf.north-pipe.ca -all` |

If you already have an SPF record, add `include:_spf.north-pipe.ca` before the `-all` or `~all` mechanism:

```
v=spf1 include:_spf.google.com include:_spf.north-pipe.ca -all
```

#### DKIM Record (CNAME)

Points to NorthRelay's DKIM signing key for your domain.

| Field     | Value                                         |
| --------- | --------------------------------------------- |
| **Type**  | CNAME                                         |
| **Host**  | `default._domainkey`                          |
| **Value** | Provided in the dashboard (unique per domain) |

#### Verification Record (TXT)

A one-time record that proves you own the domain.

| Field     | Value                                         |
| --------- | --------------------------------------------- |
| **Type**  | TXT                                           |
| **Host**  | `_northrelay-verify`                          |
| **Value** | Provided in the dashboard (unique per domain) |

### Step 3: Add Records to Your DNS Provider

Add all three records through your DNS provider's management interface. Common providers:

* **Cloudflare:** DNS > Add Record (set Proxy Status to "DNS only" for CNAME)
* **Route 53:** Hosted Zones > your domain > Create Record
* **GoDaddy:** DNS Management > Add Record
* **Namecheap:** Advanced DNS > Add New Record

DNS propagation can take up to 48 hours, though most changes take effect within 15 minutes to 2 hours.

### Step 4: Verify

1. Return to **Dashboard > Domains**
2. Click **Verify** next to your domain
3. NorthRelay checks for all three DNS records
4. On success, the domain status changes to **Verified**

## Domain States

| State        | Meaning                                    | Action                             |
| ------------ | ------------------------------------------ | ---------------------------------- |
| **Pending**  | Domain added, DNS records not yet verified | Add DNS records and click Verify   |
| **Verified** | All DNS records confirmed                  | Domain is ready to use for sending |
| **Active**   | Domain is being used for sending           | No action needed                   |
| **Failed**   | Verification check failed                  | See Troubleshooting below          |

## Using Your Verified Domain

Once verified, you can use your domain as the `from` address in API calls:

```typescript
await client.emails.send({
  from: 'notifications@yourdomain.com',
  to: ['recipient@example.com'],
  subject: 'Sent from my domain',
  html: '<p>Custom domain email!</p>',
});
```

In the dashboard, your verified domain appears as an option when composing emails.

## Managing Domains

### Viewing Domain Status

Go to **Dashboard > Domains** to see all your domains with their current verification status, DNS record status, and last verification check time.

### Re-verifying a Domain

If DNS records change or verification expires, click **Re-verify** on the domain's row in the dashboard. NorthRelay will re-check all DNS records.

### Removing a Domain

1. Go to **Dashboard > Domains**
2. Click the domain you want to remove
3. Click **Delete Domain**

Removing a domain immediately stops email sending from that domain. Any emails in queue with that domain's `from` address will fail.

## Troubleshooting

### Verification Fails: SPF Record Not Found

**Cause:** The SPF TXT record has not propagated or contains a syntax error.

**Fix:**

1. Check that the TXT record for your domain contains `include:_spf.north-pipe.ca`
2. Verify there is only one SPF record (multiple SPF records cause failures)
3. Use the [Pre-Flight Check tool](/docs/features/diagnostic-tools) to validate your DNS
4. Wait for DNS propagation (up to 48 hours) and retry

### Verification Fails: DKIM Record Not Found

**Cause:** The CNAME record for `default._domainkey` is missing or pointing to the wrong value.

**Fix:**

1. Confirm the CNAME host is exactly `default._domainkey.yourdomain.com`
2. If using Cloudflare, ensure the proxy is disabled (DNS only / gray cloud)
3. Verify the CNAME target matches the value shown in your dashboard

### Verification Fails: Verification TXT Not Found

**Cause:** The `_northrelay-verify` TXT record is missing or has the wrong value.

**Fix:**

1. Check the record host is `_northrelay-verify.yourdomain.com`
2. Confirm the value matches exactly (no extra spaces or quotes)
3. Some DNS providers add the domain suffix automatically -- enter just `_northrelay-verify` as the host

### DNS Propagation Delays

DNS changes can take up to 48 hours to propagate globally. If verification fails immediately after adding records:

1. Wait 15-30 minutes and try again
2. Use a DNS lookup tool (e.g., `dig` or a web-based checker) to confirm records are visible
3. If records are visible in DNS but verification still fails, contact support

## Related

* [Plans & Pricing](/docs/billing/plans) for tier requirements
* [Two-Pool System](/docs/features/two-pool-system) for understanding IP isolation
* [Reputation Monitoring](/docs/features/reputation-monitoring) for tracking domain health
* [Diagnostic Tools](/docs/features/diagnostic-tools) for DNS validation


# Reputation Monitoring



# Reputation Monitoring

NorthRelay continuously monitors your sender reputation and automatically detects delivery issues. Your reputation score directly affects whether your emails land in the inbox or get filtered to spam.

## Understanding Sender Reputation

Every email you send contributes to your sender reputation -- a score that mailbox providers (Gmail, Outlook, Yahoo, etc.) use to decide whether to accept, filter, or reject your messages. NorthRelay tracks this internally and provides a real-time view in your dashboard.

A high reputation means:

* Better inbox placement rates
* Fewer emails landing in spam
* Higher delivery speeds (less throttling by receivers)
* Protection of the IP pool you share with other senders

## Reputation Score

Your reputation score starts at **100** and adjusts based on your sending behavior. The score is recalculated hourly via automated snapshots.

### Score Ranges

| Range      | Status    | Meaning                                                              |
| ---------- | --------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **90-100** | Excellent | Top-tier deliverability. Keep doing what you are doing.              |
| **70-89**  | Good      | Healthy sending. Minor improvements possible.                        |
| **50-69**  | Fair      | Deliverability may be impacted. Review your practices.               |
| **30-49**  | Poor      | Significant delivery issues. Immediate action needed.                |
| **0-29**   | Critical  | Most emails will be rejected or filtered. Sending may be restricted. |

### Score Calculation

The score is computed from a base of 100 with deductions based on negative signals:

| Factor                | Weight | Description                                                        |
| --------------------- | ------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| **Bounce Rate**       | High   | Percentage of emails that hard-bounced (invalid addresses)         |
| **Spam Complaints**   | High   | Percentage of recipients who marked your email as spam             |
| **Delivery Failures** | Medium | Percentage of emails that failed to deliver (timeouts, rejections) |
| **Engagement**        | Low    | Open and click rates relative to your sending volume               |

Deductions are proportional. For example, a 5% bounce rate has a larger impact than a 1% bounce rate. The formula weights recent activity more heavily than historical data to reflect your current sending health.

## Dashboard Overview

Navigate to **Dashboard > Reputation** to see:

### Current Score

A large gauge displays your current score with color coding (green, yellow, orange, red) and the trend direction (improving, stable, declining) compared to 24 hours ago.

### Score History

A line chart shows your daily reputation score over the past 30 days. Use this to spot trends and correlate score changes with sending activity or incident events.

### Active Incidents

A list of any currently open incidents that are affecting your reputation, with severity indicators and recommended actions.

### Key Metrics

Four metric cards show your current:

* **Bounce Rate** -- with threshold indicator
* **Spam Complaint Rate** -- with threshold indicator
* **Delivery Success Rate** -- percentage of emails accepted by receivers
* **Engagement Rate** -- opens and clicks as a percentage of delivered emails

## Incident Detection

NorthRelay automatically detects problems and creates incidents when metrics cross defined thresholds.

### Incident Types

| Type                | Trigger                                     | Description                                                                    |
| ------------------- | ------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `HIGH_BOUNCE_RATE`  | Bounce rate exceeds threshold               | Too many emails are bouncing, indicating list quality issues                   |
| `SPAM_COMPLAINTS`   | Complaint rate exceeds threshold            | Recipients are marking your emails as spam                                     |
| `HIGH_FAILURE_RATE` | Delivery failure rate exceeds threshold     | Emails are failing to deliver due to server rejections or timeouts             |
| `SCORE_DROP`        | Score drops significantly in a short period | Sudden reputation decline detected, even if no single metric is over threshold |

### Severity Levels

| Severity     | Meaning                                    | Action                                                   |
| ------------ | ------------------------------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------- |
| **WARNING**  | A metric has crossed its warning threshold | Review your sending practices and contact lists          |
| **CRITICAL** | A metric has reached a severe level        | Immediate action required to prevent delivery disruption |

### Thresholds

| Metric                | Warning     | Critical    |
| --------------------- | ----------- | ----------- |
| Bounce Rate           | > 3%        | > 8%        |
| Spam Complaint Rate   | > 0.1%      | > 0.3%      |
| Delivery Failure Rate | > 5%        | > 15%       |
| Score Drop (24h)      | > 15 points | > 30 points |

### Auto-Resolution

Incidents automatically resolve when the triggering metric returns below its warning threshold. You do not need to manually close incidents. The resolution is noted in the incident history with a timestamp.

## Improving Your Reputation
